mcqs.question: Which notation provides a tight bound on algorithm complexity?
mcqs.explanation: Big-Theta (Θ) provides both upper and lower bounds, making it a tight bound. If f(n) = Θ(g(n)), f(n) grows asymptotically as fast as g(n).

mcqs.question: Binary search has time complexity:
mcqs.explanation: Binary search divides the search space in half with each comparison, resulting in logarithmic time complexity.

mcqs.question: Which time complexity is considered the most efficient?
mcqs.explanation: Logarithmic time O(log n) is more efficient than linear O(n) because growth is much slower as input size increases.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of nested loops where outer loop runs n times and inner loop runs i times for each i?
mcqs.explanation: The inner loop runs 1+2+3+...+n = n(n+1)/2 times, which is Θ(n²).

mcqs.question: If f(n) = 3n² + 2n + 1, what is the Big-O complexity?
mcqs.explanation: In Big-O notation, we keep the highest order term and drop constants. The dominant term is n².

mcqs.question: Which notation represents the worst-case analysis?
mcqs.explanation: Big-O notation provides an upper bound, representing the worst-case scenario for algorithm performance.

mcqs.question: Merge Sort has time complexity:
mcqs.explanation: Merge Sort divides the array in half (log n levels) and merges at each level (n work), resulting in O(n log n).

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of Merge Sort?
mcqs.explanation: Merge Sort requires an auxiliary array of size n for merging, giving it O(n) space complexity.

mcqs.question: Which complexity class grows slowest?
mcqs.explanation: Constant time O(1) does not grow with input size, making it the slowest growing complexity.

mcqs.question: If T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n, what is the time complexity using Master Theorem?
mcqs.explanation: Using Master Theorem: a=2, b=2, f(n)=n. Here n^(log₂2) = n¹ = n, so f(n) = Θ(n), case 2 gives Θ(n log n).

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
mcqs.explanation: Array index access is constant time because the memory address is calculated directly using the index.

mcqs.question: Which algorithm has O(n!) time complexity?
mcqs.explanation: Generating all permutations of n elements requires n! operations as there are n! possible permutations.

mcqs.question: What does Ω(1) represent?
mcqs.front: Best-case complexity is at least constant
mcqs.explanation: Omega notation provides a lower bound, representing the best-case scenario.

mcqs.question: The complexity O(n + m) is used when:

options.text: There are two different input sizes

mcqs.correctAnswer: There are two different input sizes
mcqs.explanation: When an algorithm takes two inputs of different sizes n and m, we use O(n + m) to represent combined complexity.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm has worst-case O(n²) but average O(n log n)?
mcqs.explanation: Quick Sort has O(n²) worst-case when elements are already sorted, but O(n log n) average case with randomized pivots.

mcqs.question: What is the relationship if f(n) = o(g(n))?
mcqs.explanation: Little-o notation means f(n) grows strictly slower than g(n); the ratio f(n)/g(n) approaches 0.

mcqs.question: Time complexity of Tower of Hanoi with n disks:
mcqs.explanation: Tower of Hanoi requires 2ⁿ - 1 moves, which is exponential time complexity O(2ⁿ).

mcqs.question: Which notation is used for best-case analysis?
mcqs.explanation: Big-Omega (Ω) provides a lower bound, representing the best-case scenario for algorithm performance.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of checking if a number is prime?
mcqs.explanation: To check if n is prime, we only need to check divisibility up to √n, giving O(√n) complexity.

mcqs.question: f(n) = 5n³ + 2n² + 10 has complexity:
mcqs.explanation: In asymptotic analysis, we drop lower-order terms and constants. The dominant term is n³.
